name: Juniper Marinated Medallions of Caribou Fillet
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry the crushed juniper berries in oil until they change color slightly.
2. Let cool. Marinate the caribou fillet in this oil and juniper mixture overnight in a cool place.
3. Cut the caribou fillet into steaks or medallions, about 3/4 inch thick. Trim the edges and set aside.
4. Fry the trimmings in a little oil until brown and crispy. Add chopped onion and carrot; fry gently until onions are golden brown.
5. Pour in red wine, add cloves and herbs, and reduce gently until half volume. Strain and set sauce aside.
6. Season medallions with pepper, toss in flour, and fry in butter until well-browned but pink inside. Keep warm.
7. Pour some of the sauce into the frying pan and scrape to mix in meat juices. Add cream; stir to incorporate it into the sauce without boiling.
8. Finally, add black currant jam. Pour the sauce over the medallions and serve.
